name: Trim live state inside the recovered 128x128 PTX hot-band control path

summary.name: Trim live state inside the recovered 128x128 PTX hot-band control path
summary.hypothesis: If the recovered PTX winner drops a small amount of register lifetime in the steady-state loop, achieved warps should rise above the current 16.63% plateau without reopening the round-1 256x128 regression.
summary.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue with a secondary tensor_core_underutilization component on the accepted 128x128 PTX hot-band path

score_breakdown.reason: Matches the dominant register / occupancy wall on the recovered winner while staying inside one bounded PTX-local code surface.

ranking_notes: Registers Per Thread and Achieved Occupancy are still the highest-value structured findings.
This stays on the recovered winner instead of paying another geometry-switch tax.
A small register drop can improve both active warps and tensor utilization at once.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if registers stay flat near 200 and active warps do not improve, or if barrier rises while runtime regresses.

target_hotspots.explanation: Launch Statistics is carrying metric Registers Per Thread.
Occupancy is carrying metric Achieved Occupancy.
Occupancy is carrying metric Theoretical Occupancy.

expected_local_changes: Shorten the lifetime of grouped-row / pid / future-tile temporaries around the PTX steady-state loop.
Avoid keeping both next-tile and future-tile address chains live across the accumulate call when one can be recomputed in a narrower scope.
Keep the recovered grouped-row mapping intact while trimming control-path live state.

guardrail_metrics.reason: Tensor activity is part of the active bottleneck picture and should not drop after the next code edit.
Latency-hiding is already weak; active warps should not regress.
long scoreboard stalls are consuming 7.16% of active warp issue slots.
barrier stalls are consuming 5.47% of active warp issue slots.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
